claim: Independent verification confirms the explorer claim is accurate: YOLO demand evidence dispatch API and schema already support research/fetch style evidence with WebFetch/WebSearch marked as scope=external, while still requiring project-scoped path evidence for target-project facts. The implementation correctly distinguishes external research evidence from project facts across schema, validation logic, agent prompts, tests, and doctrine.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION field definition explicitly states: 'evidence: Array of { path?, url?, line?, scope, source, summary, why } records. scope is project | external | user | unknown; project facts require project-scoped evidence.'
evidence.why: Primary schema contract evidence showing scope distinction is built into the evidence result definition

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES = new Set(['project', 'external', 'user', 'unknown']) restricts scope values to exactly four valid options
evidence.why: Code evidence proving only the four defined scope values are valid

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope classifies evidence as external when url field matches http/https pattern OR source field matches external_web, webfetch, websearch patterns
evidence.why: Core scope classification logic showing how external research evidence from WebFetch/WebSearch is detected and distinguished

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasProjectLocator validates project-scope evidence requires a concrete path; line 417 explicitly returns false if path matches http/https pattern
evidence.why: Validation logic proving URLs cannot serve as project locators even if marked as scope=project

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues generates PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker when evidence is only external/user/unknown; generates PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker when project-scope evidence lacks concrete path
evidence.why: Core validation logic enforcing external-only evidence cannot satisfy project fact requirements

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness calls evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues for each evidence result, adding any blockers to readiness.check
evidence.why: Integration evidence showing PRD readiness validation enforces external vs project evidence distinction

evidence.summary: Test 'external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts' confirms evidence with scope=external triggers PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker
evidence.why: Test evidence proving external-only evidence cannot satisfy project fact requirements

evidence.summary: Test 'external URLs cannot be relabeled as project evidence' confirms evidence with url field but marked as scope=project triggers PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker
evidence.why: Test evidence proving external URLs cannot be relabeled as project evidence even with scope=project declaration

evidence.summary: Agent prompt instructs: 'If the demand explicitly asks for external research/fetch/search, actually use WebFetch/WebSearch when available and record those records as scope=external' and 'WebFetch/WebSearch are allowed for external research; mark those records as scope=external and do not use them as project facts'
evidence.why: Agent prompt evidence showing WebFetch/WebSearch are explicitly allowed but must be marked scope=external and cannot prove project facts

evidence.summary: Doctrine states: 'project evidence must come from target项目的 code, tests, docs, config, logs or artifacts, and must carry repo-relative path or file locator; external evidence can only come from WebFetch/WebSearch or external docs, but can only be used as background research'
evidence.why: Product doctrine evidence confirming external research cannot prove target-project facts

assumptions: WebFetch and WebSearch tools are available in the agent tooling environment when research or full tool profile is active
risks: If agents ignore prompt rules about marking WebFetch/WebSearch as scope=external, the system would rely solely on post-validation blockers which may not catch all edge cases

result.notes: Independent verification confirms all key evidence from the explorer. Schema defines four scopes with project facts requiring project-scoped evidence. Router classifies external evidence from URLs and webfetch/websearch source patterns. Validation logic requires project evidence to have concrete file paths, not URLs. Tests confirm external-only evidence blocks project claims and external URLs cannot be relabeled as project evidence. Agent prompts explicitly instruct WebFetch/WebSearch usage for external research but mark as scope=external and not use as project facts. Doctrine confirms external research is background only. No conflicts found between explorer claims and independent verification.
